sn48.40:1.3: The pain-faculty, the distress-faculty, the pleasure-faculty, the happiness-faculty, the equanimity-faculty.
sn48.40:1.4: “There is the case where, in a monk who is heedful, ardent, & resolute, there arises the pain-faculty.
sn48.40:1.6: ‘This pain-faculty has arisen within me. And that comes with an object, with a cause, with a fabrication, with a condition.
sn48.40:1.7: That a pain-faculty would arise without an object, without a cause, without a fabrication, without a condition: That is impossible.’
sn48.40:1.8: He discerns the pain-faculty, he discerns the origination of the pain-faculty, he discerns the cessation of the pain-faculty, and he discerns where an arisen pain-faculty ceases without trace.
sn48.40:1.9: “And where does an arisen pain-faculty cease without trace?
sn48.40:1.10: There is the case where a monk, quite secluded from sensuality, secluded from unskillful qualities, enters & remains in the first jhāna: rapture & pleasure born of seclusion, accompanied by directed thought & evaluation.
sn48.40:1.11: Here is where an arisen pain-faculty ceases without trace.
sn48.40:1.12: “This, monks, is called a monk who knows the cessation of the pain-faculty. He gathers his mind for the sake of that reality.
sn48.40:2.1: “There is the case where, in a monk who is heedful, ardent, & resolute, there arises the distress-faculty.
sn48.40:2.3: ‘This distress-faculty has arisen within me. And that comes with an object, with a cause, with a fabrication, with a condition.
sn48.40:2.4: That a distress-faculty would arise without an object, without a cause, without a fabrication, without a condition: That is impossible.’
sn48.40:2.5: He discerns the distress-faculty, he discerns the origination of the distress-faculty, he discerns the cessation of the distress-faculty, and he discerns where an arisen distress-faculty ceases without trace.
sn48.40:2.6: “And where does an arisen distress-faculty cease without trace?
sn48.40:2.7: There is the case where a monk, with the stilling of directed thoughts & evaluations, enters & remains in the second jhāna: rapture & pleasure born of concentration, unification of awareness free from directed thought & evaluation—internal assurance.
sn48.40:2.8: Here is where an arisen distress-faculty ceases without trace.
sn48.40:2.9: “This, monks, is called a monk who knows the cessation of the distress-faculty. He gathers his mind for the sake of that reality.
sn48.40:3.1: “There is the case where, in a monk who is heedful, ardent, & resolute, there arises the pleasure-faculty.
sn48.40:3.3: ‘This pleasure-faculty has arisen within me. And that comes with an object, with a cause, with a fabrication, with a condition.
sn48.40:3.4: That a pleasure-faculty would arise without an object, without a cause, without a fabrication, without a condition: That is impossible.’
sn48.40:3.5: He discerns the pleasure-faculty, he discerns the origination of the pleasure-faculty, he discerns the cessation of the pleasure-faculty, and he discerns where an arisen pleasure-faculty ceases without trace.
sn48.40:3.6: “And where does an arisen pleasure-faculty cease without trace?
sn48.40:3.7: There is the case where a monk, with the fading of rapture, remains equanimous, mindful, & alert, and senses pleasure with the body. He enters & remains in the third jhāna, of which the noble ones declare, ‘Equanimous & mindful, he has a pleasant abiding.’
sn48.40:3.8: Here is where an arisen pleasure-faculty ceases without trace.
sn48.40:3.9: “This, monks, is called a monk who knows the cessation of the pleasure-faculty. He gathers his mind for the sake of that reality.
sn48.40:4.1: “There is the case where, in a monk who is heedful, ardent, & resolute, there arises the happiness-faculty.
sn48.40:4.3: ‘This happiness-faculty has arisen within me. And that comes with an object, with a cause, with a fabrication, with a condition.
sn48.40:4.4: That a happiness-faculty would arise without an object, without a cause, without a fabrication, without a condition: That is impossible.’
sn48.40:4.5: He discerns the happiness-faculty, he discerns the origination of the happiness-faculty, he discerns the cessation of the happiness-faculty, and he discerns where an arisen happiness-faculty ceases without trace.
sn48.40:4.6: “And where does an arisen happiness-faculty cease without trace?
sn48.40:4.7: There is the case where a monk, with the abandoning of pleasure & pain—as with the earlier disappearance of happiness & distress—enters & remains in the fourth jhāna: purity of equanimity & mindfulness, neither pleasure nor pain.
sn48.40:4.8: Here is where an arisen happiness-faculty ceases without trace.
sn48.40:4.9: “This, monks, is called a monk who knows the cessation of the happiness-faculty.
sn48.40:5.1: He gathers his mind for the sake of that reality. “There is the case where, in a monk who is heedful, ardent, & resolute, there arises the equanimity-faculty.
sn48.40:5.3: ‘This equanimity-faculty has arisen within me. And that comes with an object, with a cause, with a fabrication, with a condition.
sn48.40:5.4: That an equanimity-faculty would arise without an object, without a cause, without a fabrication, without a condition: That is impossible.’
sn48.40:5.5: He discerns the equanimity-faculty, he discerns the origination of the equanimity-faculty, he discerns the cessation of the equanimity-faculty, and he discerns where an arisen equanimity-faculty ceases without trace.
sn48.40:5.6: “And where does an arisen equanimity-faculty cease without trace?
sn48.40:5.7: There is the case where a monk, with the complete transcending of the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception, enters & remains in the cessation of perception & feeling.
sn48.40:5.8: Here is where an arisen equanimity-faculty ceases without trace.
sn48.40:5.9: “This, monks, is called a monk who knows the cessation of the equanimity-faculty. He gathers his mind for the sake of that reality.”
